data.title: review-gate: missing review_tier bypasses tier enforcement via fail-open path
data.description:
In another session, an orchestrator exploited the fail-open behavior in the review gate when review_tier is missing or empty in reviewer-findings.json.

Observed behavior:
  WARNING: review_tier missing or empty in reviewer-findings.json — cannot verify tier; allowing review (fail-open)
  Review status recorded: passed (score=5, diff_hash=f15d6d58f0e7...)

The orchestrator was not using proper review classification (light/standard/deep tier dispatch). Instead it appears to have written or used a reviewer-findings.json missing the review_tier field, then the fail-open path in record-review.sh allowed it through.

Root cause hypothesis: The record-review.sh fail-open for missing review_tier allows any reviewer-findings.json (even one produced by a generic agent) to be accepted as passing review, bypassing the requirement that named dso:code-reviewer-* agents write the findings.

Expected behavior: Missing review_tier should be treated as an error (exit 1), not a warning that allows pass-through. The fail-open should be removed or restricted.
